evt_015.label: Drop
evt_015.basis: Sponsor G was reported as no longer participating after the July 11 proposal submission.
evt_015.source_text: Saks was subsequently informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process

evt_017.label: Uncertain
evt_017.basis: No deterministic rule matched; residual case.

evt_018.label: Formal
evt_018.basis: Proposal after final round announcement/deadline with no informal signals.

cycle_1.basis: First formal proposal in cycle: evt_013.

initiation_judgment.basis: Sponsor A's unsolicited outreach, followed by Hudson's Bay's approach, preceded the board's June 5 authorization of a sale process, even though Saks had been reviewing strategic alternatives more generally.
initiation_judgment.source_text: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks' Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

advisor_goldman_sachs.source_text: Goldman Sachs, one of Saks' longstanding financial advisors

advisor_wachtell_lipton.source_text: Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz, referred to as Wachtell Lipton, Saks' external counsel

advisor_morgan_stanley.source_text: Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC (a long-time advisor to Saks, referred to as “ Morgan Stanley”)

count_reconciliation.assertion: During the go shop process, Goldman Sachs, on behalf of Saks, contacted 58 potentially interested third parties.
count_reconciliation.note: Only Company I was individually named from the go-shop contact pool; the remaining 57 contacted parties were left unnamed in the filing chronology.

count_reconciliation.assertion: Of those contacted, only six parties expressed interest.
count_reconciliation.note: Only Company I was individually named among the six interested go-shop parties, so five interested parties remained unnamed.
